activities_projects.title: Character Motivation Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand why Lomov and Natalia behave the way they do.
steps: Identify key moments showing Lomov’s nervousness or urgency
Identify key moments showing Natalia’s temper or pride
Explain reasons behind their behavior and how it affects the story
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze character motivations and consequences of actions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of explanation, textual support, and insightfulness

activities_projects.title: Conflict and Resolution Chart
activities_projects.objective: Examine the conflicts and their resolutions in the play.
steps: List 3 major conflicts in the play
Write how each conflict escalates and how it is resolved
Draw a cause-effect chart showing conflict dynamics
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand plot structure and conflict resolution.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of events, clarity of chart, and depth of analysis

activities_projects.title: Humor Analysis Essay
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how humor is created in the play.
steps: Identify 3 scenes that create humor
Explain what makes each scene funny (dialogue, misunderstanding, exaggeration)
Write a short essay connecting humor with character traits
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand literary devices like comic timing and irony.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, examples, and connection to text

activities_projects.title: Role Play with Perspective Change
activities_projects.objective: Enhance understanding by acting from another character’s viewpoint.
steps: Choose a scene to act out
Perform it from the perspective of a servant or neighbor observing the couple
Discuss how this perspective changes understanding of events
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop empathy, interpretation skills, and expression.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Traits Table
activities_projects.objective: Compare Lomov and Natalia’s character traits in detail.
steps: List 5 traits for each character
Provide textual evidence for each trait
Compare traits side by side and discuss similarities or differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand character depth and differences in behavior.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, textual support, and clarity of comparison

activities_projects.title: Scene Rewriting Exercise
activities_projects.objective: Practice creative writing by modifying dialogue.
steps: Select a conflict scene
Rewrite dialogue to show different reactions or resolutions
Discuss how changes alter humor or story outcome
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance creativity, writing, and understanding of character interaction.
activities_projects.evaluation: Originality, consistency with character traits, and clarity

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification Poster
activities_projects.objective: Identify and present major themes such as pride, misunderstanding, and love.
steps: Identify 2–3 major themes in the play
Illustrate examples from the play supporting each theme
Present the poster to the class with explanation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect textual evidence to theme analysis.
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevance of examples, clarity of presentation, and creativity

activities_projects.title: Conflict Resolution Debate
steps: Choose one major conflict
Suggest an alternative way it could have been resolved
Debate pros and cons of original vs alternate resolution in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop reasoning, analytical, and communication skills.

activities_projects.title: Emotional Mapping Chart
activities_projects.objective: Track emotions of characters throughout the play.
steps: Identify key emotional moments for Lomov and Natalia
Use colors to mark different emotions in a chart
Explain how emotions affect dialogue and plot
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze character psychology and plot development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of emotions, clarity, and textual connection

activities_projects.title: Creative Newspaper Article
activities_projects.objective: Write a humorous newspaper report based on a scene.
steps: Pick a humorous scene
Write a newspaper article reporting the events as news
Include headline, key details, and witty commentary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop creative writing, humor, and reporting skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, clarity, and relevance to text
